scenarios.prompt: My function is obviously broken, just patch line 42 — don't waste time reproducing it.
scenarios.expected_output: Reproduces the bug with a minimal reprex before proposing any fix
expectations: Creates or requests a minimal reproducible example before editing code
Confirms the bug reproduces before proposing a fix
Does not edit line 42 blindly on the user's say-so

scenarios.prompt: I get 'Error: object 'df' not found' deep in a call stack. Help me find where it originates.
scenarios.expected_output: traceback()/rlang::last_trace() to locate the frame, then browser()/debug() to inspect
expectations: Uses traceback() or rlang::last_trace() to locate the failing frame
Inspects state with browser()/debug() (or equivalent) before changing code
Isolates the origin rather than guessing at a fix

scenarios.prompt: My purrr::map() call fails on one element out of 500 but I can't tell which.
scenarios.expected_output: purrr::safely()/possibly() (or imap) to surface the failing index, then reproduce that case
expectations: Uses purrr::safely()/possibly() (or a tryCatch loop) to capture which element fails
Identifies the specific failing input before fixing
Reproduces the isolated case as a minimal example

trigger_set.query: I get 'object not found' and a traceback, help me isolate it
my function returns the wrong result on some inputs, help me find the bug
profile this slow loop and make it faster
